Patch Set 8: Code-Review+1

I'm not against this patch fixing the client to do better error handling, but it is definitely bad if the MDS depends on the client to do the right thing.  This will also be a problem if the MDS is running with an older client that doesn't have this fix.

In general, a bug on the server should not be fixed with a patch on the client, which is why I didn't think that this patch could possibly be related to LFSCK working correctly on the server.  It seems like this is a bug introduced by http://review.whamcloud.com/13392

Patch Set 8:

It is not the patch http://review.whamcloud.com/13392 introduced some bug, but because we hidden such bug by some other issues before.

As for asking client to handle server side trouble, that is not a good idea. I agree with you for that. I will consider more.

Patch Set 11:

> I'd say that the commit message is misguiding a bit. this problem exists no its own and can be seen w/o LFSCK (leaked space, for example).

Yes, it someone calls lu_object_put_nocache() on such object that is referenced by the leaked open handle, it will also cause LU_OBJECT_HEARD_BANSHEE set.

parents.subject: LU-6280 lod: delete xattr on striped dir

commit.subject: LU-6301 llite: cleanup open handle for client open failure
commit.message:
LU-6301 llite: cleanup open handle for client open failure

For open case, the client side open handling thread may hit error
after the MDT grant the open. Under such case, the client should
send close RPC to the MDT as cleanup; otherwise, the open handle
on the MDT will be leaked there until the client umount or evicted.

If the LFSCK marks LU_OBJECT_HEARD_BANSHEE on the MDT-object that is
opened by others for repairing some inconsistency, such as repairing
multiple-referenced OST-object, because the leaked open handle still
references the MDT-object, then it will block the subsequent threads
that want to locate such object via FID.

Signed-off-by: Fan Yong <fan.yong@intel.com>
Change-Id: I1fff2cde179b039e3bee562ef79d5cf3587fe3c8
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/13709
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Lai Siyao <lai.siyao@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Alex Zhuravlev <alexey.zhuravlev@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
